name: Deforestation Detection & Driver Classification from Satellite Imagery
description: Research using deep learning and remote sensing to detect, forecast, and attribute forest loss, including classifying the drivers of deforestation (e.g. mining, cattle ranching, agriculture). Spans model development, dataset creation, and deployment in specific geographies like the Amazon and Indonesia.

name: Forest Carbon Stock Estimation & Carbon Credit Integrity
description: Work applying ML to quantify forest carbon sequestration, estimate carbon stocks, and establish deforestation baselines for carbon credits and Payments for Environmental Services. A recurring concern is transparency, verification, and correcting overestimation to ensure integrity of climate finance instruments.

name: Land Use / Land Cover Classification & Change Detection
description: Methods for semantic segmentation and classification of land cover from satellite and aerial imagery, including change detection over time and scalable pipelines for mapping. Frequently applied to specific regions (Kigali, Karachi, India mining sites) to characterize land-use transitions.

name: Individual Tree & Canopy Structure Measurement
description: Techniques for measuring physical forest attributes at fine granularity—tree canopy density and height, DBH estimation, tree reconstruction, and dead tree segmentation—using satellite, UAV, mobile, and depth-sensing data. Supports inventory, monitoring, and urban forestry applications.

name: Foundation Models & Data-Efficient Learning for Earth Observation
description: CS-methods research advancing transferable and label-efficient techniques for remote sensing: visual prompt tuning, few-shot/weakly-supervised learning, geographic location encoders, data programming, and cross-modal transfer. Cuts across many application domains by tackling shared bottlenecks in labeled data and generalization.

name: Vegetation & Ecosystem Dynamics Forecasting
description: Predictive modeling of vegetation greenness, phenology, drought resilience, and localized climate impacts using spatio-temporal deep learning and mechanistic-vs-data-driven comparisons. Focused on forecasting future ecosystem states rather than mapping current conditions.

name: Community Co-Design & Indigenous Knowledge in Climate Monitoring
description: Ethnographic and participatory ICTD work engaging local and Indigenous communities in designing environmental sensing and knowledge systems, including microclimate sensors and digitizing traditional knowledge for climate resilience. Emphasizes barriers, equity, and situated deployment over pure model performance.

name: Interpretability & Verification for Climate Finance and Conservation
description: Systems making geospatial ML predictions auditable and trustworthy for conservation payments and climate finance, through explainability, metric-learning verification of conservation projects, and transparent communication tools. Bridges model design with the sociotechnical demand for accountability in payment schemes.
